description: Turn replacement or ROI messaging into a simple interactive calculator that makes platform consolidation or savings feel concrete.
evidence_notes: Notion uses a savings calculator with checkboxes and a team-size input to make consolidation value tangible.
rules: Model one business claim numerically instead of leaving it abstract.
Keep the number of inputs small and meaningful.
Show the result immediately without requiring form submission.
implementation_notes: Use lightweight client-side state derived from checkbox or quantity inputs.
Provide clear defaults so the module communicates value before any interaction.
Use semantic labels, checkbox groups, and number inputs with accessible helper text.
avoid_when: The business value cannot be estimated honestly with a simple model.
The result would feel obviously inflated or marketing-only.
The calculator introduces more friction than explanatory clarity.
